en: When you sit down to eat with a ruler, observe carefully what is before you,
zh: 你與官長一起坐席吃飯的時候， 要特別留意在你面前的是誰。

en: and put a knife to your throat if you are given to appetite.
zh: 你若是個貪吃的人， 就當把刀子放在喉嚨上。

en: Do not desire his delicacies, for they are deceptive food.
zh: 不可貪戀他的美食， 因為那是騙人的食物。

en: Do not toil to acquire wealth; be discerning enough to desist.
zh: 不要勞碌求富， 你要明智地放下這企圖。

en: When your eyes light on it, it is gone, for suddenly it sprouts wings, flying like an eagle toward heaven.
zh: 你的眼睛注視在錢財上，錢財卻不見了， 因為錢財必長起翅膀， 如鷹飛往天上。

en: Do not eat the bread of a man who is stingy; do not desire his delicacies,
zh: 不可吃吝嗇人的飯， 也不可貪戀他的美食。

en: for he is like one who is inwardly calculating. "Eat and drink!" he says to you, but his heart is not with you.
zh: 因為他心裡怎樣計算，他的為人就是怎樣； 他雖然對你說：“請吃，請喝！” 他的心裡卻沒有你。

en: You will vomit up the morsels that you have eaten, and waste your pleasant words.
zh: 你所吃的那一點食物，必要吐出來， 你道謝的美言，也都白費了。

en: Do not speak in the hearing of a fool, for he will despise the good sense of your words.
zh: 不要說話給愚昧人聽， 因為他必藐視你明慧的言語。

en: Do not move an ancient landmark or enter the fields of the fatherless,
zh: 你不可遷移古時的地界； 也不可侵佔孤兒的田地。

en: for their Redeemer is strong; he will plead their cause against you.
zh: 因為他們的救贖主大有能力， 他必向你為他們的案件伸冤。

en: Apply your heart to instruction and your ear to words of knowledge.
zh: 你要專心領受教訓， 留心聽知識的言語。

en: Do not withhold discipline from a child; if you strike him with a rod, he will not die.
zh: 要管教孩童，不可姑息， 你雖然用杖打他，他也不會死；

en: If you strike him with the rod, you will save his soul from Sheol.
zh: 你要用杖打他， 就可以救他的靈魂免下陰間。

en: My son, if your heart is wise, my heart too will be glad.
zh: 我兒，如果你的心有智慧， 我的心也就歡喜；

en: My inmost being will exult when your lips speak what is right.
zh: 你的嘴唇說正直話的時候， 我的內心就歡樂。

en: Let not your heart envy sinners, but continue in the fear of the LORD all the day.
zh: 你的心不要嫉妒罪人， 只要時常敬畏耶和華。

en: Surely there is a future, and your hope will not be cut off.
zh: 因為萬事必有結局， 你的盼望也不會斷絕。

en: Hear, my son, and be wise, and direct your heart in the way.
zh: 我兒，你要聽，要有智慧， 要引導你的心走在正路上。

en: Be not among drunkards or among gluttonous eaters of meat,
zh: 酗酒的人，不可與他們來往； 暴食的人，不要與他們為友。

en: for the drunkard and the glutton will come to poverty, and slumber will clothe them with rags.
zh: 因為酗酒暴食的人必致窮乏； 貪睡的人必穿破衣。

en: Listen to your father who gave you life, and do not despise your mother when she is old.
zh: 你要聽從生你的父親； 也不可因為母親老了，就藐視她。

en: Buy truth, and do not sell it; buy wisdom, instruction, and understanding.
zh: 你要買真理，不可出賣； 要得智慧、教訓和哲理。

en: The father of the righteous will greatly rejoice; he who fathers a wise son will be glad in him.
zh: 義人的父親必大有快樂； 生下智慧的兒子的，必因兒子歡喜。

en: Let your father and mother be glad; let her who bore you rejoice.
zh: 要使你的父母歡喜， 使生下你的快樂。

en: My son, give me your heart, and let your eyes observe my ways.
zh: 我兒，把你的心給我； 你的眼目要關注我的道路。

en: For a prostitute is a deep pit; an adulteress is a narrow well.
zh: 因為妓女是深坑， 淫婦是狹窄的井。

en: She lies in wait like a robber and increases the traitors among mankind.
zh: 她像強盜一樣埋伏， 使人間增添很多奸詐的人。

en: Who has woe? Who has sorrow? Who has strife? Who has complaining? Who has wounds without cause? Who has redness of eyes?
zh: 誰有禍患？誰有愁苦？ 誰有紛爭？誰有怨言？ 誰無故受傷？ 誰的眼睛赤紅？

en: Those who tarry long over wine; those who go to try mixed wine.
zh: 就是那些沉湎於酒， 常去品嘗調和了的酒的人。

en: Do not look at wine when it is red, when it sparkles in the cup and goes down smoothly.
zh: 你不要看酒怎樣發紅， 在杯中怎樣閃爍誘人， 喝下去怎樣舒暢，

en: In the end it bites like a serpent and stings like an adder.
zh: 最後它卻像咬你的蛇、 像刺你的毒蛇。

en: Your eyes will see strange things, and your heart utter perverse things.
zh: 你的眼睛必看見怪異的事， 你的心必說乖謬的話。

en: You will be like one who lies down in the midst of the sea, like one who lies on the top of a mast.
zh: 你必好像躺在海中， 又好像躺在桅杆頂上。

en: "They struck me," you will say, "but I was not hurt; they beat me, but I did not feel it. When shall I awake? I must have another drink."
zh: 你必說：“人打我，我不痛； 人鞭打我，我不曉得； 我甚麼時候清醒？ 我還要再去尋酒。”
